Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

What is a myth?

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2008-02-02 · 3 answers
Are Ramayana/Mahabharata a myth?

What are historical evidences?
user [1] · 2008-02-04
In reference to Ramayana, that happened millions of years ago, as nobody can prove or negate, it is up to your personal judgement.
Mahabaharata has archaeological findings backing up its historical footing:
http://www.archaeologyonline.net/artifacts/scientific-verif-vedas.html
http://www.hvk.org/articles/0204/159.html
user [2] · 2008-02-04
In reference to Ramayana, that happened millions of years ago, as nobody can prove or negate, it is up to your personal judgement.
Mahabaharata has archaeological findings backing up its historical footing:
http://www.archaeologyonline.net/artifacts/scientific-verif-vedas.html
http://www.hvk.org/articles/0204/159.html
user [38] · 2008-02-04
There is even something on Ramayana (part 1/3): http://www.zeenews.com/articles.asp?aid=405659&sid=ZNS&sname=EXCLUSIVE-NEWS
